error.signature: MongoNetworkTimeoutError: connection timed out; Socket operation timed out after 30000ms

verdict.summary: Socket or connection timeout during MongoDB operation. Slow query, network issue, or server under heavy load.

dead_ends.why_fails: Infinite timeout means a stuck connection hangs forever, eventually exhausting the connection pool.

dead_ends.action: Increase connection pool size assuming timeouts mean pool exhaustion
dead_ends.why_fails: More connections to an overloaded server makes it worse. Fix the slow queries or scale the server.

workarounds.action: Identify and optimize slow queries causing timeouts
workarounds.how: db.currentOp({ 'secs_running': { $gt: 10 } })  # find slow operations; db.setProfilingLevel(1, { slowms: 100 })

workarounds.action: Set appropriate timeout values for different operation types
workarounds.how: socketTimeoutMS=60000; connectTimeoutMS=10000; serverSelectionTimeoutMS=15000  # in connection string

workarounds.action: Check network path between application and MongoDB
workarounds.how: ping mongo-host; traceroute mongo-host; check security group rules and VPC peering if in cloud
